title: TFIND-002: Session creation still required host identity
description:
Area: client-sdk / host boundary.

Status: resolved (#332, 2d474c70d).

Triage: cat-1 (rubric anchor — client-only frontend hits host-identity wall immediately).

Firegrid.sessions.createOrLoad required CurrentHostSession because it created a host-bound RuntimeContext row through insertLocalRuntimeContext. That made remote-client-shaped tests impossible through the public session creation API: the client had to be composed with host identity, even though a production client should not be in the same Effect environment as a host.

CLUSTER RESOLUTION (Gurdas 2026-05-18, #332 merged — consolidated client/host boundary, Option 1):
- sessions.createOrLoad writes a RuntimeContextRequestRow (full public runtime intent) — no CurrentHostSession on the client path.
- Folded TFIND-001/003/038/039 into the same transaction.
- session.start writes a durable RuntimeStartRequestRow; host-side control-request-reconciler binds contexts + claims/runs starts.
- RuntimeContextRequestRow carries argv/env/agent protocol/MCP — arbitrary public runtime intent expressible client-side.
- CLI/factory migrated to host-owned synchronous startRuntime same-transaction (no compat wait-helper).
- Abandon semantics = terminal/no-revival/10min, idempotent claim via insertOrGet, duplicate-start suppression — guarded by 5 named deterministic failure-mode tests + 6th determinism test; CI-confirmed green; no cast on reconciler/claim/dedup/completion path.
- TFIND-004/008 unblocked: durable-intent/reconciler seam makes separate-process client/host e2e expressible.
close_reason: Resolved by #332 — consolidated client/host boundary, Option 1 (client writes durable intent; host reconciler claims starts).

title: TFIND-048: MCP route + URL lifecycle ownership unresolved in #332 model
description:
Area: client/host boundary / MCP route + URL lifecycle.

Status: open — REFRAMED 2026-05-18 (Reading 2 / architectural; was mis-shaped as 'client-sdk missing helper'); framing-gated, folds into the #332-impl MCP-lifecycle question; blocks Codex ACP.

Triage: cat-1/2 architectural (real production-model gap, not a missing API: re-exporting sessionContextIdForExternalKey would canonize the backwards lifecycle = WRONG fix. The determinism primitive itself is sound; the smell is URL-lifecycle ownership. Migration-as-validation working exactly as designed — proved #332 left an MCP-lifecycle hole. NOT cat-3 toy-fix).

The #332 client/host model does not resolve who builds the concrete contextId-scoped MCP URL and when. Codex ACP bakes it client-side pre-createOrLoad; production (host-sdk/mcp-host.ts, CLI) has the host own the MCP server with a /mcp/runtime-context/:contextId route resolved at tool-call time. Client-baking a concrete URL into the intent is test-fixture-shaped.

Evidence (file:line):
- sessionContextIdForExternalKey (protocol/src/session-facade/schema.ts:482) is sound deterministic primitive for client/host independent reconciliation, NOT for clients to pre-bake route URLs.
- Production host owns MCP server: host-sdk/src/host/mcp-host.ts:1-26 mounts /mcp/runtime-context/:contextId; route param is request authority, resolved at tool-call time (:118-124), no pre-provisioning.
- Codex ACP test/config builds concrete URL pre-createOrLoad and embeds in intent — test fixture starts server before context exists.
- SDD_CONSOLIDATED_CLIENT_HOST_BOUNDARY.md:49-95 is silent on who builds the concrete contextId-scoped MCP URL and when.
- No other config uses MCP or pre-derives contextId-scoped URL. Codex ACP is unique.

Resolution path: architectural framing decision on MCP route/URL lifecycle in #332 model. Codex ACP stays paused/unmigrated; resumed migration is the validation.
close_reason: DECIDED: Reading 2 / Option A. Host provisions the contextId-scoped MCP route URL and delivers it at start; the protocol carries a URL-less runtimeContextMcp marker on schema so clients structurally cannot express the host-owned fact. sessionContextIdForExternalKey remains the determinism primitive for client/host reconciliation, not a client-side route-URL pre-bake. CLI co-located pre-bake is the documented exception, not the model. Five conditions ratified and honored in PR #344: (1) host-scoped FiregridRuntimeContextMcpBaseUrl Option-typed primitive with single construction site, late-bound on bind; (2) URL-less runtimeContextMcp marker on protocol schema; (3) single injection site at codec-adapter resolveEffectiveMcpServers, with CLI pre-createOrLoad deletions confirmed; (4) Codex ACP resume validates as deterministic gating seam with zero real-LLM dependence — no it.skip, no test-side shortcuts, real-LLM e2e demoted to non-gating smoke (not deleted); (5) doc-commented SINGLE-PURPOSE primitive — do not generalize. Deterministic-seam restructure complete via oca2; CI green across all five gates. Dependents tf-7u6 (codex-acp-tool-call-pipeline config) and tf-kdf (TFIND-051: codex-acp re-runnability) unblock on close.

title: Projection surfaces: client SDK and CLI presets over protocol launch/channel contracts
description:
Generalize Firegrid consumer surfaces as projections over protocol-owned substrate/channel contracts, not as separate orchestration APIs. This includes @firegrid/client-sdk, @firegrid/cli, future REST/gRPC/JSON-RPC bindings, and ergonomic agent presets such as `--agent codex` / `--agent claude`.

Ground truth:
1. `packages/client-sdk/README.md` documents @firegrid/client-sdk as writing durable session intent and reading durable projections. Its canonical create path is `sessions.createOrLoad({ runtime: local.jsonl(...) })`.
2. `packages/cli/src/bin/run.ts` currently lowers decoded argv to `local.jsonl({ argv: config.agentArgv, ...present })`.
3. `docs/cannon/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_SCHEMA_PROJECTION_CONTRACT.md` defines protocol -> bindings -> execution, where agent tools, client SDK, and CLI bind the same protocol contracts without importing execution substrate.
4. `docs/cannon/architecture/host-sdk-runtime-boundary.md` names client-sdk, host-sdk, and CLI as peer bindings over protocol schemas.

Target:
1. Treat client-sdk as the TypeScript/app projection of protocol operations and durable channel/session observations.
2. Treat CLI presets as command-line projections of the same launch/session contracts, compiling to `local.jsonl(...)` / protocol `RuntimeConfig` plus host-owned runtime-context MCP/channel attachments.
3. Make room for future REST, gRPC, JSON-RPC, or MCP bindings as additional projections over the same operation catalog.
4. Do not introduce a central graph/orchestration API; projections expose choreography primitives and observations.

Examples:
1. TypeScript: `sessions.createOrLoad({ runtime: local.jsonl({ argv: [...], agentProtocol: ... }) })`.
2. CLI: `pnpm firegrid -- run --agent codex --prompt ...` lowers to the same protocol launch intent.
3. Future REST/gRPC/JSON-RPC endpoints validate against the same protocol operation schemas and delegate to binding/execution services rather than cloning contracts.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Client SDK README and root README explain client/CLI/REST/gRPC/JSON-RPC as projections over protocol contracts, not separate graph APIs.

description:

Projection-scope clarification (Gurdas Q): the observation element RuntimeAgentOutputObservation is a protocol projection so SEMANTICS are plane-neutral and would carry — BUT TFIND-040 decided the CLIENT binding only; an agent-tool binding of subscribe is a SEPARATE, not-yet-made decision. (2) The internal helper `projectionWait` (packages/client-sdk/src/internal/projection-wait.ts) is `stream.pipe(Stream.filter(pred), Stream.runHead, Effect.asVoid)` — a SINGLE-SHOT match-and-return Effect, NOT a continuous Stream. description:
Per Gurdas's SDD architecture-tightening series (main commits e81bd6ba4, 92ec6a295, d00e12cf7).

PRINCIPLE: Channels are generic typed facades over durable operator streams. NOT a parallel data model (registry-as-service). NOT workflow handles. The application model is Effect Context.Tag + Layer per channel — never a Registry object that hands out things.

CANONICAL SHAPE (per SDD_FIREGRID_AGENT_BODY_PLAN.md, d00e12cf7):

class LinearWebhookChannel extends Context.Tag('firegrid/channels/linear.webhook')<
  LinearWebhookChannel,
  IngressChannel<LinearWebhook>
>() {}

const LinearWebhookChannelLive = Layer.effect(
  LinearWebhookChannel,
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    const table = yield* LinearWebhookTable        // private infra
    return makeIngressChannel(table.rows())        // ProjectionStream<LinearWebhook>
  })
)

Below session plane (hidden from agent/app code):
- DurableTable / ProjectionStream / CDC mechanics / collection names
- Workflow engine / execution ids / scoped engines
- String lookup tables (allowed ONLY at MCP/protocol-edge adapter)

Above session plane (what agent/app code sees):
- Named typed channels via Effect Context.Tag
- Layer-provided capabilities (yield* SomeChannelTag)
- wait_for(channel-string, { match, timeoutMs }) verb (string→Tag resolution at protocol boundary)

WHAT TO RESTRUCTURE:

(1) tf-lawq ChannelRegistry-service shape → REMOVE the ChannelRegistry service. Each channel becomes its own Tag.
(2) tf-bhv9 state.changes Slice C.3 → typed Tag generic over collection row type, Layer wraps DurableTable.rows()
(3) tf-fmwg event(name) Slice C.2 → typed Tag generic over event payload, Layer wraps event-stream substrate
(4) tf-v8i4 approval Slice C.4 → typed CallableChannel<ApprovalRequest, ApprovalResponse> Tag
(5) tf-mybr dm/notification Slice C.5 → typed Tag pair per handle
(6) tf-xl6k session.log Slice C.6 → typed EgressChannel<LogRow> Tag
(7) tf-gnp1 session.self.* Slice C.1 → typed Tag pair for lifecycle/checkpoint
(8) tf-dlaa wait_for(channel) tool dispatch → restructure to resolve string → Tag at protocol-edge adapter; yield* TagSpecific to consume
(9) tf-w88b channel metadata in MCP tools/list → derive from registered Tags at host composition time (not from a Registry instance)

WEBHOOK-AS-CHANNEL GENERALIZATION:
Any inbound source (webhooks, CDC streams, event tables, agent-emitted events, lifecycle signals) gets the SAME agent-facing shape: wait_for(channel, match, timeoutMs). Diversity lives in Layer compositions at host startup, not in agent vocabulary.

ACCEPTANCE:
- ChannelRegistry service tag removed
- Each Phase-2 channel slice exposes its own typed Context.Tag + Layer
- Host composition merges all channel Layers; no central registry instance exists
- MCP-edge adapter does string → Tag resolution; produces a typed Effect that yields the Tag's capabilities
- Application/business/tool code receives typed channel capabilities via Layer; NEVER a registry, never workflow handles, never substrate coordinates
- rg pattern: zero hits for direct ChannelRegistry imports outside the MCP-edge adapter

NOT YET DISPATCHED. Queued post-Phase-1-fan-in. This is the TRUE body-plan surface; current Phase-2 channel shape is registry-scaffold that lands functionality but with anti-Effect-idiom abstraction layer.

Cross-refs:
- d00e12cf7 (SDD: frame channels over durable operators)
- 92ec6a295 (SDD: align channels with effect layers)
- e81bd6ba4 (SDD: codify channel workflow boundary)
- tf-ws2x (runtime-context-engine-registry collapse — analogous Layer-only direction)
- tf-0mt5 (engine-native primitives escape hatch — informs streamWait/streamWaitAny shape)
close_reason: DECIDED: completed by PR #502; ChannelRegistry service replaced by ChannelInventory/per-channel Tags + Layer composition. Remaining channel unification work is now tracked under the One Substrate Primitive validation epic.

- Each channel becomes its own Context.Tag<XChannel, IngressChannel<Row>> (or Egress/Callable)
- Channel-specific Layer.effect(XChannel, makeXChannelImpl) compositions
- Tool dispatch yields the Tag directly (yield* XChannel) rather than registry.require
- MCP-edge string→Tag adapter is the only allowed lookup; lives strictly at protocol boundary

title: GAP(dark-factory choreography): agent-driven factory loop not expressible on current public surface
description:

CLASSIFICATION: (b) NARROW FIREGRID SURFACE-EXPOSURE GAP — NOT a protocol mismatch. Triage: cat-1/2, P1. Resolution = scoped PRODUCTION SURFACE FIX (sidecar/SDD), NOT an architect protocol-redesign binary.

EVIDENCE: PR #365 / commit f1002df45 / packages/tiny-firegrid dark-factory-pipeline.smoke.test.ts (passing).

PRECISE GAP SET (exactly three):
1. RESUME: ACP spec + SDK support session/load (replays full conversation via session/update before response); Firegrid AcpSessionLive / public session facade calls session/new ONLY, never session/load, and AgentSession.meta exposes no ACP wire sessionId to store/reload. → FIX: expose session/load + surface the ACP wire sessionId.
2. WAIT SCHEMA: wait_for schema accepts RuntimeRun/AgentOutput sources only → caller-owned DurableTable approval-fact waits not expressible. → FIX: extend wait_for schema to caller-owned facts.
3. EXECUTE: execute tool is exposed but the live host execute is unsupported. → FIX: wire host execute.

CORRECTION (narrowed): delegation is NOT a gap — session_new/session_prompt are exposed and sufficient. The earlier 'no spawn/spawn_all delegation gap' / 'unsupported execute/spawn_all/cancel/close genuinely-absent' framing is STRUCK. The only execute-related gap is item 3 (execute tool exposed, host side unwired); spawn_all/cancel/close are NOT gaps (delegation via session_new/session_prompt covers it).

title: dark-factory sim rebuild: Linear webhook ingest + factory-vision choreography over channel surface
description:
Per Gurdas's directive 2026-05-20 + factory-vision.md.

REBUILD packages/tiny-firegrid/src/simulations/dark-factory/ to demonstrate the factory-vision narrative over the channel surface that just landed in Phase 2.

CURRENT STATE: dark-factory has driver.ts + host.ts + index.ts but uses the OLD wait substrate (HostOwnedDurableToolsWaitForLive) and was built before channels existed. It's a placeholder.

TARGET STATE: a working sim that runs end-to-end through Firegrid's public CHANNEL surface (no reach-pasts) and demonstrates §7's 7 capabilities via §6's choreography pattern.

KEY ELEMENTS:

(1) LINEAR WEBHOOK INGEST CHANNEL:
- Register IngressChannel<LinearWebhook> via ChannelRegistry per the canonical pattern (d00e12cf7)
- The channel's substrate is a DurableTable.rows() projection (collection of LinearWebhook rows)
- Sim's host seeds the table with simulated webhook deliveries (since this is a sim, not real Linear)
- Planner (driver) calls wait_for('linear.webhook', { match: { action: 'issue.created' }, timeoutMs }) — exactly the agent-facing shape in the SDD

(2) FACTORY CHOREOGRAPHY (per factory-vision §6):
- Planner reads incoming Linear ticket via wait_for(linear.webhook)
- Planner decides next move (e.g., 'this needs clarification' OR 'plan immediately')
- Planner produces a plan, then call(approval, { plan }) — uses approval channel for human gate
- On approval: planner delegates implementation via session_new + session_prompt OR via send(event('implementer.start'), ...)
- Implementer comes back with PR → planner spawns reviewers (multi-agent choreography via event(name))
- Reviewers respond → planner moves to merge or feedback loop
- On merge: planner watches CI via wait_for + merges

(3) CAPABILITIES TO DEMONSTRATE (per factory-vision §7):
- (1) External events as durable verified facts → ingress channel from sim-seeded LinearWebhook table
- (2) Participant identity durable across time → session-handle correlation
- (3) One external intent → one participant → sessions.createOrLoad keyed by ticket external key
- (4) Participants delegate to other participants → planner spawns implementer/reviewers
- (5) Participants wait for things → wait_for(channel) + wait_for_any
- (6) Participants take actions and remember → session.log channel (Slice C.6)
- (7) Everyone sees what's happening → trace/observability spans

CHANNELS TO REGISTER (per SDD/Slice C.* on main):
- IngressChannel<LinearWebhook> ('linear.webhook') — the trigger
- CallableChannel<ApprovalRequest, ApprovalResponse> ('plan.approval') — human gate via tf-v8i4
- event(name) channels ('implementer.started', 'reviewer.verdict', 'ci.status') — agent-to-agent + machine events via tf-fmwg
- session.self.lifecycle / session.self.checkpoint — interoception (tf-gnp1)
- session.log — efferent annotation (tf-xl6k)
- dm/notification — human channels (tf-mybr)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Sim runs end-to-end against a real claude-agent-acp planner
- Trace shows: linear.webhook ingress fires → planner decides → approval call → delegation → events flow → terminal state reached
- NO reach-pasts: every primitive the planner uses goes through public channel surface (no DurableTable references in driver, no engine references, no wait-router references)
- §7 capabilities exercised + observable in trace
- pnpm run verify green

KNOWN CONSTRAINTS:
- Use CURRENT channel surface on main (ChannelRegistry-shape). The Phase-3 refactor (tf-kddg) will move this to Tag+Layer per channel — accept the registry-shape as scaffold for now.
- tf-xw0w may still be in flight; if so, build against current main (substrate cutover doesn't change agent-facing surface).
- Channels above, workflows/substrate below — don't expose DurableTable, ProjectionStream, engine, or stream URLs in driver code.

EXPLICIT NON-SCOPE:
- Real Linear integration (this is a sim; seed the LinearWebhook table directly)
- Real GitHub PR / CI (sim-seed events)
- Real Slack (sim notifications channel)
- Refactoring ChannelRegistry shape (that's tf-kddg)

ESTIMATED: substantial, ~half-day to day.

REFERENCE READ:
- docs/vision/factory-vision.md (the narrative)
- docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_DARK_FACTORY_APP.md (the contract)
- docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_AGENT_BODY_PLAN.md (channel definitions + IngressChannel<LinearWebhook> example)
- packages/tiny-firegrid/src/simulations/dark-factory/ (current scaffold to rebuild)
- packages/tiny-firegrid/src/simulations/inv5-cross-agent-event-choreography/ (multi-agent pattern from INV-5)
- packages/host-sdk/src/host/channel-registry.ts (current channel surface)
- packages/host-sdk/src/host/channels/* (the Phase-2 channel bindings)

This is the production proof of factory-vision per §5: 'when a factory runs end-to-end through Firegrid's public surface without reaching into private internals, the substrate has earned its claim.'

title: INV-6: tiny-firegrid sim — Activity α/β/γ shape comparison (validates future Activity-rethink SDD direction)
description:

title: demo P0: thin ACP-shim forced first Firegrid tool_call for §6 planner (live §6 or hardened terminal finding)
close_reason: DECIDED: hardened terminal finding ACCEPTED — exemplary, not papered, decision-grade negative evidence. The thin ACP-shim (additive opt-in, substrate untouched) PROVABLY engaged (session_update ~6x) yet codex-acp still issued ZERO Firegrid tools/call (one codex-exploration title only), s6 0/6. Architectural boundary: an ACP-layer shim can refuse prose-only completion and re-drive the turn but CANNOT make the model CHOOSE a tool — tool_choice:required lives in the agent-internal model-request, unreachable from the ACP protocol layer. Combined with tf-549/#422 (native: no ACP runtime nor the ACP protocol exposes forced tool-invocation, source-verified) and tf-9q4 (cross-runtime), the conclusion is FULLY HARDENED & TERMINAL: Firegrid §6 substrate is provably complete; live agent-driven §6 is blocked SOLELY on agent-side forced tool-invocation that is, by construction, unreachable from the ACP protocol layer — a fundamental ACP-architecture/third-party limitation, NOT a Firegrid defect. Only path to a live full §6 = a non-ACP planner invocation with native tool_choice:required = a PO/Gurdas decision, not autonomous, not blocking the (complete, honest, research-grade) demo. oca3 shim angle CLOSED as negative evidence.

title: TFIND-031: Shared DurableTable tag-family provision missing (root cause)
description:
Area: host/toolkit composition.

Status: resolved (#331, e48d82904).

Triage: cat-2 (real host-composition correctness — missing shared DurableTable tag-family provision, was masked by the TFIND-005 any).

Surfaced by TFIND-005. Initially filed as 4 separate leaks (TFIND-031..034); Agent 2's diagnosis (2026-05-17) showed they are ONE root cause: all four production boundaries plus test fallout leak the same tag family (RuntimeControlPlaneTable, RuntimeOutputTable, and four DurableWait* row tags). The any collapsed every consumer's requirements channel, so a single missing provision site in the host/toolkit composition manifested at 4 production type-boundaries + test boundaries.

Resolution: Option-Y execution-scoped self-containment — support layer required what it provided. Fix (NO cast): provide the SAME HostRuntimeObservationSubstrateLive reference INTO the executor while keeping it provideMerged into the workflow chain → Effect memoization = ONE shared store (workflow body + wait-router + tool executor); toolCallWorkflowSupportLayer got analogous self-containment.

A first sibling-Layer.merge attempt typechecked but broke TOOL_EXECUTOR_SEAM.2 schedule_me — caught and rejected by deterministic record→blocked→wake test, not forced green; corrected re-thread passes it. Full gate green; closes the TFIND-005 leak-stack root.
close_reason: Resolved by #331 — Option-Y execution-scoped self-containment; one root cause, four manifestations folded in.
